title: Tim Sippel, Ph.D.
text:
Senior Marine and Aquatic Scientist


I am a senior environmental scientist, specialising in aquatic and marine ecology. My 25+ years of experience includes the last 10 years working in the Pacific Northwest on salmonid science and population management with state, Federal, and tribal co-managers. The range of projects I have worked on include dam relicencing, dam removal, population dynamics including survival analysis and abundance estimation, spawning habitat restoration, and ESA section 7 consultations in Washington, Oregon, California and Alaska.

title: Tim Sippel, Ph.D.
text:
Contact

tsippel@gmail.com |  Website |  LinkedIn |  GitHub |  Download PDF

title: Tim Sippel, Ph.D.
text:
Work History
Stantec, Senior Environmental Scientist - Fish Ecology SME: Jul 2024 - Present









Position: Senior scientific and analytical support for regulatory compliance projects including hydroelectric dam relicensing and removals, Biological Assessments, ESA Section 7 consultations, habitat restoration and mitigation, onshore and offshore renewable energy, site remediation, water quality, water management and conveyance, and fishery management. Data analytic and statistical support for diverse projects.


Accomplishments: Senior scientist on projects including salmon spawning habitat supplementation, fish passage, Contribute to procurement of new business and contracts, mentor junior staff, senior authorship of peer-reviewed publication, member of National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine editorial committee and California Central Valley Project Improvement Act (CVPIA) Technical team









Worley, Senior Environmental Scientist - Fish Ecology SME: Jun 2022 - Jun 2024









Position: Senior scientific and analytical support for regulatory compliance projects including offshore renewable energy, climate change risk assessment, salmon population assessment, assessment of fishing within global marine protected areas


Accomplishments: Contributed to securing new business and contracts, mentored junior staff, presented at scientific conferences for offshore renewable energy and marine ecology









Washington Department of Fish and Wildlife, Fishery Scientist: Aug 2017 - Aug 2022









Position: Monitoring, forecasting and reporting on salmon population abundance for fishery compliance with the Endangered Species Act and US vs Oregon Management Agreement. Close coordination with other state, Federal and tribal fishery managers. WDFW lead representative to US vs Oregon Technical Advisory Committee.


Accomplishments: Initiated and lead data modernization and analysis projects to improve transparency, accuracy and efficiency. Contributed new thought leadership to technical issues on data management and analysis with direct application to agency obligations and priorities.









Ocean Associates (NOAA Contractor), Research Biologist: Sep 2011 - Jul 2017









Position: Conducted fishery stock assessments as a member of international and domestic scientific working groups. Served as NMFS representative to the Pacific Fishery Management Council’s Highly Migratory Species Management Team advising on fishery management policy.


Accomplishments: Made key scientific contributions to both domestic and international fishery management as stock assessment scientist and scientific advisor. Senior authorship of two peer-reviewed scientific publications.









University of Hawaii, Post-doctoral Scientist: Mar 2010 - Aug 2011









Position: Develop models to estimate behavior of fish carrying satellite transmitters, and contribute to development and deployment of databases to manage satellite tagging data.


Accomplishments: Developed tools for analyses of animal movement ecology, including R codebases and Access databases for large electronic tagging datasets. Co-authored a peer-reviewed book chapter on juvenile white sharks.









Blue Water Marine Research (New Zealand), Fishery biologist: Jan 2004 - Jan 2010









Position: Analyze data, write technical reports, organize and support field research


Accomplishments: Managed satellite tagging projects, lead data analyses, helped bring in new projects









Monterey Bay Aquarium, Research Technician: Nov 1999 - Jan 2002









Position: Organize and support field and laboratory research


Accomplishments: Managed research aquarium facility, organized logistics for field research, contributed to research at-sea

title: About
text: This website hosts Tim Sippel’s CV. I will will be expanding my site more soon…

title: Website
text:
← Back to CV

Portland Harbor Site Remediation (Oregon) — Geostatistical analysis of surface and subsurface harbor sediment toxicity for assessment of EPA compliance.
Burnt River stream stabilization project (Oregon) — Permitting, construction design and lead on-site aquatic work to rebury pipeline exposed in stream channel
Marathon Palladium Project (Canada) — Support development of statistical analyses and reporting for groundwater monitoring at mine project
US Navy Base - Bremerton/Kitsop Multi Mission Dry Dock Design (Washington) — Evaluate project work windows to minimize the risk to aquatic species inlcuding bull trout, green sturgeon, and others.
Eklutna Lake Hydroelectric Project (Alaska) — Retrofit Eklutna Lake reservoir, near Anchorage, to enable fish passage, and envaluate feasibility of improvements to salmonid spawning habitat
Foster Fish Ladder Improvement Project (Oregon) — Biological Assessment and Environmental Impact Assessments for US Army Corp project to install pipeline to raise water temperatures to encourage salmon passage
Potter Valley Dam Removal (California) — Environmental impact assessment for removal of Scott and Cape Horn dams on the Eel River in Potter Valley California
Enloe Dam Removal (Washington) — Feasibility study of environmental impacts to aquatic resources from Enloe Dam removal
Spring Chinook habitat supplementation (California) — Assess Chinook spawning habitat carrying capacity of Feather River habitat supplementation project to evaluate regulatory compliance.
Subsea Fibre Optic Cable Permit Feasibility Study (California; Hawaii) — Permitting, regulatory and environmental compliance assessment for subsea fiber optic cable project
Yanacocha Lagoon ecological impact compen project (Peru) — Develop an aquatic habitat compensation plan to offset loss of Yanacocha Lagoon due to mine tailings being deposited into the lagoon. The effects of the lagoons loss will be offset by either developing a new lagoon nearby, or enhancing the productivity of other nearby lagoons. The area is home to endemic pupfish (genus Orestias) which are rare and live in highly specialized environments.
Western Water Project (US West Coast) — Environmental and fishery impact assessment for project proposing to convey freshwater from the Columbia River through a subsea aqueduct that would run down the US West Coast to Central California.
Offshore Wind Development Planning in Oregon Call Areas (Oregon) — Permitting roadmap, fishery and tribal impact assessment of offshore wind off of Oregon
Assessment of suitability of catch and release recreational fishing in Marine Protected Areas (Global) — Analysis of regulatory and scientific requirements for recreational fisheries in global marine protected areas.
Oregon Production Index Coho Population Assessment (Pacific Northwest) — Developed tools built in R to automate analysis and documentation of coho salmon abundance, fishery impact assessment and forecasting.
Environmental assessment of offshore wind in Pacific US Territories (Pacific Islands) — Produced a report assessing monitoring needs for marine life during offshore wind development in US Pacific, including Hawaii and territories.
Community Offshore Wind (New Jersey) — Develop fishery protection plan for offshore wind project off of New Jersey
Climate Change risk assessment — Developed a toolbox in R for climate change risk assessments using IPCC forecast models.
Novacastrian Wild (Australia) — Drafted marine biological survey and commercial fishery risk plans offshore wind project off of New South Wales, Australia
